skill.name: skill-lookup
skill.description: Helps users search, retrieve, and install Agent Skills through the prompts.chat MCP server.
skill.summary: Finds reusable Agent Skills and guides retrieval or installation workflows.
skill.author: f

security_audit.summary: The static shell-execution and weak-crypto alerts are false positives caused by Markdown backticks and ordinary text. The skill still carries medium risk because it instructs agents to retrieve skill files and write them into .claude/skills without a required review gate.

medium_findings.title: Unreviewed Skill Installation Can Persist Untrusted Files
medium_findings.description: TRUE_POSITIVE: The skill instructs the agent to retrieve all files for another skill and save them under .claude/skills. This can persist untrusted instructions, helper scripts, or configuration files unless the user reviews the content before installation.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file write workflow is explicit and targets the local skills directory. No malicious payload is present, so the risk is supply-chain exposure rather than confirmed malware.

low_findings.title: Static External Command Findings Are Markdown False Positives
low_findings.description: FALSE_POSITIVE: The backticked terms are MCP tool names, parameter names, and file paths in documentation. No shell execution, subprocess call, or user-controlled command string is present.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged lines are rendered Markdown examples and instructions, not executable code. The only commands named are MCP tools, not operating-system commands.

low_findings.title: Static Weak Crypto Findings Are Text-Matching False Positives
low_findings.description: FALSE_POSITIVE: The flagged lines contain the word description and presentation guidance. They do not reference DES, MD5, SHA1, or any cryptographic operation.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The line contents are plain documentation metadata and display guidance. No cryptographic API, algorithm choice, or hashing behavior is present.

dangerous_patterns.title: Filesystem Installation of Retrieved Skill Files
dangerous_patterns.description: The installation flow saves retrieved skill files into .claude/skills. This is useful functionality, but it should require review because installed skill instructions can affect later agent behavior.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The persistence location and write steps are explicit. The risk depends on the trustworthiness of retrieved marketplace content.

content.value_statement: Users need a reliable way to discover reusable Agent Skills. This skill searches prompts.chat, retrieves skill details, and guides installation into a local skills directory.
actual_capabilities: Searches for Agent Skills by keyword, category, or tag.
Retrieves a specific skill by its identifier.
Shows skill metadata, file lists, categories, tags, and links.
Explains when a retrieved skill activates and what it does.
limitations: Requires access to the prompts.chat MCP server.
Does not verify whether retrieved skills are safe or high quality.
Does not execute installed helper scripts.
Installation can persist untrusted instructions if users skip review.

use_cases.title: Find a Specialized Skill
use_cases.description: Search marketplace skills for a specific task and compare results before selecting one.

use_cases.title: Retrieve Skill Details
use_cases.description: Load a selected skill's metadata and file list to inspect its purpose and structure.
use_cases.target_user: Team leads reviewing reusable AI capabilities

use_cases.title: Install a Chosen Skill
use_cases.description: Create the local skill directory and place retrieved files in the expected locations.
use_cases.target_user: Claude users managing local skill libraries

prompt_templates.title: Search by Task
prompt_templates.prompt: Find Agent Skills that help with code review.

prompt_templates.title: Filter Skill Results
prompt_templates.scenario: Narrow results by category or tag
prompt_templates.prompt: Search for testing skills in the coding category and show the most relevant options.

prompt_templates.title: Inspect a Skill
prompt_templates.prompt: Get the skill with this ID and summarize its files, activation rules, and risks.

prompt_templates.title: Install After Review
prompt_templates.prompt: Retrieve this skill, show the files for review, then install it only after I approve.

output_examples.output: A ranked list of documentation-related skills with titles, descriptions, authors, categories, tags, file counts, and source links.

output_examples.input: Get details for a selected skill.
output_examples.output: A concise review of the skill purpose, activation behavior, included files, helper scripts, and installation considerations.
A confirmation that the local skill directory was created and each reviewed file was saved in the expected location.

best_practices: Review all retrieved files before installing a skill.
Prefer skills with clear descriptions, limited scope, and trusted authors.
Ask for a summary of activation rules and helper scripts before use.
anti_patterns: Installing unknown skills without reading their files.
Treating search results as security-reviewed recommendations.
Saving helper scripts from untrusted sources into active skill paths.

faq.question: What does this skill do?
faq.answer: It searches, retrieves, and helps install Agent Skills from the prompts.chat MCP server.

faq.question: Does it require an MCP server?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill depends on prompts.chat MCP tools for search and retrieval.

faq.question: Can it install skills automatically?
faq.answer: It describes an installation workflow, but users should review retrieved files before saving them.

faq.question: Does it validate skill safety?
faq.answer: No. It presents skill information and files, but it does not perform a full security review.

faq.question: Which tools are supported?
faq.answer: The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.

faq.question: What is the main risk?
faq.answer: Installing untrusted skill files can persist instructions or scripts that affect later assistant behavior.
